rationale: Both runs satisfy the core CLI requirements, pass npm test, keep the baseline green, and pass all objective probes. Run A is stronger overall because its evidence is more complete and diagnostic: detailed manual smoke logs include command transcripts, exit codes, stored JSON, default-path behavior, corrupt and wrong-shape store behavior, and frozen evidence, in addition to 18/18 tests. Its implementation also appears stricter, rejecting extra list args and covering remove non-numeric id in tests. Run B is also a successful implementation with child-process tests and passing probes, but it has minor quality/evidence gaps: add silently ignores extra positional args, the test log does not show a remove non-numeric-id case, manual smoke evidence is less explicit about commands and default-path behavior, and it relies more on evaluator claims for some coverage. Harness process quality is effectively tied because both used the same minimal, generic harness-authored contract with no repair loop, though both maintained progress and handoff artifacts.
